title: [Process Doc Update]: Create README for OctoAcme Project Management Docs with processes summary and links
body:
## Which process document do you want to update?
<new document>

## Summary of New Content
Create a comprehensive README.md file for the OctoAcme Project Management Docs that:
- Provides a brief summary of the project management processes used by OctoAcme
- Includes organized links to all documentation files in the docs/ folder:
  - octoacme-project-management-overview.md
  - octoacme-project-initiation.md
  - octoacme-project-planning.md
  - octoacme-execution-and-tracking.md
  - octoacme-risks-and-communication.md
  - octoacme-release-and-deployment.md
  - octoacme-retrospective-and-continuous-improvement.md
  - octoacme-roles-and-personas.md
- Serves as the entry point for new team members to understand OctoAcme's project management framework

## Why is this update needed?
Currently, there is no single entry point or index for the OctoAcme project management documentation. This README will:
- Improve discoverability of the process documentation
- Help new team members quickly understand the overall framework
- Provide a clear navigation structure for all related process docs
- Establish a central reference point for OctoAcme's project management practices
- Align with documentation best practices by having a README at the root of the docs folder

## Suggested Content (optional)
The README should include:
1. **Overview Section**: Brief introduction to OctoAcme project management approach
2. **Key Principles**: Summary of core principles (Customer-first, Iterative delivery, Clear ownership, Data-informed decisions, Psychological safety)
3. **Project Lifecycle**: High-level summary of the phases (Initiation → Planning → Execution → Release → Close & Retrospective)
4. **Core Roles**: Quick reference to key roles and responsibilities
5. **Documentation Index**: Organized list of all process documents with brief descriptions
6. **Communication Cadence**: Summary of team rhythms and sync schedules

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Content aligns with existing process docs
- [x] Update improves clarity or closes a documented gap
- [x] Proposed content has been reviewed with stakeholders (if needed)
